description.content:
Retrieves role assignments for the specified provider. Supports OData filtering
to query eligible, active, or specific role assignments.

Common filters:
- `status eq 'Active'` - Active assignments only
- `status eq 'Eligible'` - Eligible assignments only
- `roleDefinitionId eq '{id}'` - Assignments for a specific role
- `subjectId eq '{id}'` - Assignments for a specific user
OData filter expression
Maximum number of results to return
Number of results to skip
OData order by expression
OData expand expression (e.g., linkedEligibleRoleAssignment,subject,roleDefinition)
(Required) The PIM provider type (This can only be one of aadroles,azureResources,aadgroup)

description.content:
Retrieves all role definitions available for the specified resource.
For Entra roles, this returns all directory roles. For Azure resources,
this returns Azure RBAC roles applicable to the resource.

description.content:
Retrieves PIM settings for all roles on the specified resource. Settings
include activation requirements (MFA, justification, ticket info),
assignment rules (max duration, permanent eligibility), and notification
configuration.

description.content:
Retrieves security alerts generated by PIM for the specified resource.
Alerts include issues like roles with too many permanent assignments,
unused eligible roles, and potential security risks.

For example, for a "too many permanent admins" alert, each incident
represents one permanent admin assignment.

(Required) The PIM provider type (This can only be one of aadroles,azureResources,aadgroup)

The Entra PIM API is used by the Microsoft Entra Privileged Identity Management portal
(entra.microsoft.com → Identity Governance → Privileged Identity Management) for managing
just-in-time privileged access, including:

  - Entra Role Assignments (eligible, active, expired)
  - Entra Role Assignment Requests (activation, assignment, removal)
  - Entra Role Definitions and Settings
  - Azure Resource Role Assignments
  - Group-based PIM Assignments
  - PIM Alerts and Audit History
  - Discovery of Unmanaged Resources

All endpoints are served from `api.azrbac.mspim.azure.com` and require a valid
Azure AD bearer token with PIM-related permissions.

**Note:** This specification was generated through browser traffic analysis of the
Entra portal. Some endpoints may require specific PIM licensing (Entra ID P2/Governance).
Endpoints returning 403 indicate insufficient permissions; 404 may indicate the feature
is not enabled for the tenant.
